text.title: decomposition-laravel Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decomposition-laravel as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Action calling another Action inline in the same request transaction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-Action orchestration belongs in a Service class. Actions calling Actions span unclear transaction boundaries and become untestable in isolation.

failure-mode.description: Pattern collapse: agent adds business logic directly to the controller when asked to "just add an endpoint quickly".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock the convention in CONTRIBUTING.md and reference it in every system prompt. Without enforcement, agents regress to fat controllers within one sprint.

failure-mode.description: DTO drift: same field added to CreateUserDTO and User::$fillable with different names (first_name vs firstName).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce snake_case in DB, camelCase only in DTOs with an explicit mapper layer. Two agents writing parallel fields cause silent data loss.

failure-mode.description: Resource leaks fields via $this->all() or spread operators.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always explicit field lists in Resources; all() exposes password_hash, remember_token, soft-delete columns.

failure-mode.description: Action executing void — def execute(): void.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Downstream queue jobs and console commands cannot compose void Actions. Always return the canonical entity or result object.

failure-mode.description: Agents invent new layers: "Manager", "Coordinator", "Handler" without an ADR.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Whitelist allowed namespaces (Actions, Services, Repositories, DTOs, Policies) and block PRs introducing new ones.

failure-mode.description: Pattern collapse under deadline pressure. When asked to "just add an endpoint quickly," agents skip the Action layer and put logic in the controller.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock the convention in CONTRIBUTING.md and reference it in every system prompt.

failure-mode.description: DTO drift. Two agents add the same field to CreateUserDTO and User::$fillable with different names (first_name vs firstName).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce snake_case in DB, camelCase in DTOs only if you have a mapper layer.

failure-mode.description: Resource leaks soft-deleted/hidden fields. Agent generates Resource with $this->all() instead of explicit field list.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Never use all(), toArray(), or spread operators in Resources.

failure-mode.description: Action doing two things. "CreateUserAction" also sends an email and assigns a role.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Acceptable if wrapped in a transaction with intent; not acceptable if email failure rolls back the user. Force agents to declare side-effects in the docblock.

failure-mode.description: Test-per-Action explosion ignored. Agents skip tests for Actions assuming the Controller test covers them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The Controller test does NOT cover Action edge-cases. Require `actions/*/*.php` ↔ `tests/Feature/Actions/*/*.php` parity.

failure-mode.description: Form Request bypass. Agents call Action directly from a console command without re-validating; invariants the Form Request enforced are now skipped.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Move invariant checks into the Action's first 3 lines.

failure-mode.description: Agents invent new layers. "Manager", "Coordinator", "Handler" classes appear after a refactor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Whitelist allowed namespaces (Actions, Services, Repositories, DTOs, Policies) and block PRs introducing new ones without an ADR.

failure-mode.description: Decomposition without DI. Agents `new CreateUserAction()` inside the controller.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use constructor injection so tests can mock; otherwise the pattern's testability gain evaporates.

failure-mode.description: Repository confusion. Pattern includes a Repository layer optionally; agents add it everywhere "for consistency".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Either adopt repos for all aggregates or for none — partial adoption is the worst case.

failure-mode.description: Action returns void. Agents emit `public function execute(): void` and mutate state; downstream agents can't compose.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always return the canonical entity or a result object.
